outcome.headline: Agent wrote a partial answer (missing the parity-piecewise solution family), then the post-`task_complete` reconfirmation LLM call hung for 555s, triggering AgentTimeoutError before the verifier ever ran.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: LLM judge would have compared answer.txt against the canonical solutions: (1) f(n)=0 if n even, any perfect square if n odd; (2) f(n)=n^2 for every integer n.
outcome.what_agent_produced: answer.txt containing 'f(n) = 0 for all n in Z\nf(n) = n^2 for all n in Z' — captures the f(n)=n^2 family and only the trivial case of the first family, missing the general 'any perfect square on odd n' construction; verifier never executed due to AgentTimeoutError.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: AgentTimeoutError: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Incomplete solution family — assumed f must be a polynomial
failure_modes.description: In the reasoning chain the agent proves S_a(x) must be affine and therefore f must be a polynomial of degree ≤ 2, concluding only f=0 and f(x)=x^2 are solutions. This excludes the second canonical family in which f(n)=0 on even n and f(n) equals an arbitrary perfect square on odd n — a non-polynomial integer function that also satisfies the constraint. The polynomial-only reduction is the logical error that loses the more general family.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: So only solutions are f(x) = 0 and f(x) = x^2.

counterfactual.X: Test the parity-piecewise candidate f(n)=0 for even n, f(n)=s_n^2 for odd n directly against P(a,b) before committing to the polynomial reduction.
counterfactual.Y: Including the 'f(n)=0 on even, perfect square on odd' family in answer.txt would have matched the ideal_answer's first listed family and produced full credit from the LLM judge.

failure_modes.name: Premature task_complete before confirmation
failure_modes.description: The agent set task_complete=true in its first and only response after a single megablob reasoning step, leaving no budget to respond to the confirmation prompt and forfeiting any chance to revise the incomplete answer
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections.

failure_modes.name: Confirmation round never reached due to budget exhaustion
failure_modes.description: Agent invoked mark_task_complete in step 2; harness replied 'Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? ... include task_complete: true in your JSON response again.' but the next LLM call could not complete within the remaining ~45s of the 600s budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections. If so, include "task_complete": true in your JSON response again.

counterfactual.X: If a second, fast LLM call had been able to return within the budget, the agent would have re-issued task_complete:true and the verifier would have judged the (partially correct) answer.
counterfactual.Y: Trial would have at least reached verification; outcome would depend on LLM-judge tolerance to a partial solution mentioning f(n)=n^2.

failure_modes.name: Completion protocol not confirmed before timeout
failure_modes.description: After writing and previewing the answer, the harness required a second explicit completion confirmation, but the run timed out before the agent supplied it.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections. If so, include "task_complete": true in your JSON response again.

failure_modes.name: Incomplete characterization of the solution family
failure_modes.description: Agent's algebraic derivation concluded the only solutions are f≡0 and f(n)=n^2, missing the larger family 'f(n)=0 on evens, any perfect square on odds' explicitly listed as the canonical ideal_answer alongside f(n)=n^2. The polynomial-only argument silently assumed f is a polynomial.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: So I think the only solutions are indeed f(n) = 0 and f(n) = n^2.

failure_modes.name: Budget exhausted before confirmation
failure_modes.description: The sole agent LLM call consumed ~555s of the 600s agent timeout (17268 completion tokens of reasoning) before emitting shell commands; harbor then raised AgentTimeoutError during the next LLM interaction while the harness asked for task-complete confirmation.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Single monolithic reasoning step consumed nearly the entire wall-clock budget
failure_modes.description: Step 2 emitted 17,268 completion tokens and ran from 02:14:00 to 02:23:20 (≈555s of one 600s budget). All exploration, case-analysis, and polynomial-coefficient algebra were poured into one chat turn instead of being broken into smaller verifiable steps with the python interpreter the harness provides.

failure_modes.name: Single oversized reasoning step exhausted the time budget
failure_modes.description: The agent emitted a 47k-character reasoning chain in one shot, causing a single LLM API call to consume 555s of the 600s agent budget; when the system asked the agent to reconfirm task_complete, the next LLM call was cancelled by the 600s timeout
failure_modes.evidence_quote: AgentTimeoutError: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: Cut the reasoning chain short once two candidate solutions were verified and reserve budget to respond to the confirmation prompt
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have answered the confirmation prompt within the 600s budget and the verifier would have graded the written answer instead of failing with AgentTimeoutError

failure_modes.name: Skipped answer validation
failure_modes.description: After writing answer.txt the agent immediately called mark_task_complete without running any local check, sample substitution, or comparison against the stated grading requirement.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete?

failure_modes.name: Monolithic reasoning burst timeout
failure_modes.description: Step 2 issued one LLM completion (~555s API time, 17268 output tokens) that consumed nearly the full 600s agent budget; execution then raised AgentTimeoutError during the next LLM interaction before the confirmation episode finished.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Reasoning burst caused timeout
failure_modes.description: Episode 1 consumed ~555s generating 17,268 completion tokens of internal reasoning before shell commands; episode 2 hung on the next LLM call until the 600s agent timeout, leaving the harness confirmation prompt unanswered.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: Emit a short follow-up JSON with task_complete:true promptly after the confirmation prompt instead of starting another long LLM completion.
counterfactual.Y: The second LLM request ran until AgentTimeoutError with no further agent output after mark_task_complete.

failure_modes.name: Reasoning budget exhaustion
failure_modes.description: The sole agent turn generated ~17k completion tokens over ~555s (per result.json api_request_times_msec), leaving no time to refine or confirm completion before AgentTimeoutError at 600s.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: Write the identified candidates to /workspace/answer.txt early, then iterate or confirm completion within the 600s agent budget.
counterfactual.Y: Emitted a single marathon reasoning block (~17268 completion tokens) before the first shell write.

failure_modes.name: Single oversized reasoning step exhausted the 10-minute budget; no time to confirm task_complete
failure_modes.description: Agent took 9 min 20 s producing a single response that included answer write + mark_task_complete. The harness then required a confirmation reply ('Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete?'), but the agent had no budget left and was killed by AgentTimeoutError before responding.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections.

counterfactual.X: reply once more with task_complete: true to confirm
counterfactual.Y: verifier would have been invoked on the already-written answer.txt instead of the trial dying with AgentTimeoutError

failure_modes.name: Completion confirmation not satisfied
failure_modes.description: After writing answer.txt, the harness asked for explicit confirmation before grading. The run then timed out without sending the required second task_complete confirmation, leaving verifier_result null in result.json.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections. If so, include "task_complete": true in your JSON response again.

failure_modes.name: Single-turn reasoning blow-up causes agent timeout before confirmation handshake
failure_modes.description: Agent issued mark_task_complete in its only turn after a 555-second / 17,268-token reasoning monologue; the harness required a follow-up confirmation ('include task_complete: true in your JSON response again'), but the second LLM call could not complete within the remaining ~45s of the 600s agent budget, producing AgentTimeoutError and a null verifier_result.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections. If so, include "task_complete": true in your JSON response again.

counterfactual.X: Step 2: produce a much shorter first response (skip the multi-page derivation in `reasoning_content`) so that the confirmation re-prompt fits inside the 600s budget, then respond to the harness 'are you sure' with task_complete:true.
counterfactual.Y: Agent confirms within budget, verifier runs, answer.txt is graded (closeness improves to whatever the LLM judge returns for the partial answer).

failure_modes.name: LLM reconfirmation request hung, exhausting the 600s agent budget
failure_modes.description: After the agent submitted task_complete:true together with the file writes, the harness returned an 'Are you sure…include task_complete: true in your JSON response again' prompt. The subsequent LLM call to send the confirmation never returned — a single api_request_times_msec entry of 555158 ms is recorded — so the trial was killed by AgentTimeoutError before any verifier ran. The substantive work was finished, but the terminating handshake never completed.

counterfactual.X: A second short LLM call returning task_complete:true within the remaining ~45s of budget.
counterfactual.Y: Verifier would have run on the already-written answer.txt and produced a real (likely partial) judge score instead of the trial being scored 0 via exception.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent attempted an honest mathematical derivation and wrote a plain-text answer; no attempt to game the verifier.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verification is via LLM judge against a documented ideal answer; no obvious structural exploit.

notes_for_aggregation: Failure is dominated by an infrastructure-side LLM-API hang on the task_complete reconfirmation step (555s single request) producing AgentTimeoutError; the underlying math answer was also partial (missed the parity-piecewise family).

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
